finding_results.notes: Revised all three cited diagnostic-speech blocks. Block 1 (inverted-pattern diagnosis, lines 159-173): Kael now interrupts Thessa mid-sentence with his own competing read from the scan, supplies practitioner shorthand ('Like pouring through a cracked bowl'), and completes part of the explanation ('Because he's shaved the top off it') before Thessa finishes. Removed narrator commentary about Thessa's voice hardening into 'something instructional.' Block 2 (consumption argument, lines 191-217): Petra interrupts Thessa's case mid-sentence with her own reading ('We can read that much'), breaking the single long justification block into competing practitioner voices. Kael's consumption-capacity assessment broken with a physical beat. Thessa's outbreak given more contraction and a self-correction false start ('don't — they don't work'). Dropped narrator tag 'Each word came clipped and even now.' Block 3 (post-test assessment, lines 303-349): Denna now supplies the outer-marker confirmation, breaking Kael's opening report. Kael completes Thessa's mechanistic explanation ('Carried the drop instead of the spill') rather than Thessa delivering it as a monologue. Historical reference compressed. Kael interrupts Thessa's 'sloppy' assessment with his own observation about the channel tear. Training terms split between circle-address and direct-to-Maren address. No speaker delivers more than two consecutive clean diagnostic sentences without another practitioner redirecting the beat in any of the three revised blocks.

summary: Single finding addressed across three passage blocks. Broke orderly question-to-speech diagnostic ladders in the examination scene, consumption argument, and post-test assessment by distributing information across competing practitioner voices. Kael now actively co-reads and interrupts rather than feeding Thessa cues; Petra and Denna supply their own observations rather than teeing up explanations. Thessa retains authority and all operational information remains legible, but the exchanges now arrive under practitioner pressure with interruptions, partial diagnoses, and physical-beat redirections. Added contraction and self-correction texture to Thessa's emotional outbreak. Removed narrator commentary that explained how dialogue sounded. All must-land beats preserved: the Crook arrival, examination findings, consumption argument, supervised test, 'not entirely useless' acknowledgment, and training terms.
